zh.lifeSummary: 當Hannah Clingan於大約1760年出生在美國賓夕法尼亞州時，她的父親George Clingan41歲，母親Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick41歲。她於1778年與Thomas Kincaid在美國賓夕法尼亞州波藍德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1830年之前在美國田納西州。

nameMeanings.name: Clingan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Kirkcudbrightshire) and Irish (Fermanagh, Down): shortened form of Mac Clingan, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gilla Fhinnéin ‘son of the servant of (Saint) Finnian’, also Anglicized as Mac Alingen.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.
Pennsylvania was always against slavery, even though the first settlers, including Penn, came with slaves. Slavery was not prominent in the area.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Pennsylvania Historical & Museum Commission- An Act for the Gradual Abolition of Slavery

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.

historicEvents.title: Harrisburg Becomes the State Capital
historicEvents.description: Harrisburg had important parts with migration, the Civil War, and the Industrial Revolution.
historicEvents.urlTitle: United States History-History of Harrisburg Pennsylvania

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Making States Equal
historicEvents.description: The Missouri Compromise helped provide the entrance of Maine as a free state and Missouri as a slave state into the United States. As part of the compromise, slavery was prohibited north of the 36°30′ parallel, excluding Missouri.

en.lifeSummary: When Hannah Clingan was born about 1760, in Pennsylvania, United States, her father, George Clingan, was 41 and her mother, Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick, was 41. She married Thomas Kincaid in 1778, in Cumberland, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died before 1830, in Tennessee, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Clinganが約1760年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州で生まれた時，父George Clinganは41歳，母Margaret Jane Kirkpatrickは41歳であった。1778年にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州クウンバーランドでThomas Kincaidと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1830年以前にアメリカ合衆国テネシー州で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Clingan(이)가 대략 1760년 미국 펜실베이니아 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 George Clingan(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1778년 미국 펜실베이니아 주 c벌랜드에서 Thomas Kincaid(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1830년 이전 미국 테네시 주에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hannah Clingan родилась около 1760 г. в местности Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени George Clingan было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Jane Kirkpatrick, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Kincaid в 1778 г. в местности кумбирланд, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла перед 1830 г. в местности Теннесси, Соединенные штаты.
